source:
import { auth } from "@/auth"; import { redirect } from "next/navigation"; import Link from "next/link"; import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; export default async function Home() { const session = await auth(); if (session?.user) { redirect("/dashboard"); } return (
{/* Hero */}
◆ Provenance

Transparent AI Writing Verification

Write with AI assistance and prove exactly how much. Provenance tracks every AI interaction, paste event, and writing session — then lets you share a verified badge anyone can audit.

{/* How it Works */}

How it Works

{/* Writer flow */}

For Writers

  1. 1. Write in the editor with full AI assistance — inline suggestions, side panel chat, or freeform generation.
  2. 2. Every keystroke, AI call, and paste event is automatically tracked with origin marks.
  3. 3. Preview what will be public, then generate a verifiable badge with one click.
  4. 4. Embed the badge in your article, blog post, or portfolio.
{/* Reader flow */}

For Readers

  1. 1. Click the Provenance badge on any verified document.
  2. 2. See exactly what percentage was AI-generated vs human-written.
  3. 3. Expand the full audit timeline — every AI prompt, response, and editing session.
  4. 4. Trust the content because the process is transparent.
{/* Footer */}
Provenance — Transparent AI writing verification
); }

source:
export function ScopeStatement() { return (
What this badge certifies
This badge certifies that the writing process for this document was tracked using Provenance. It records AI interactions, paste events, and writing sessions to provide transparency about how the content was created.
How the AI percentage is calculated
AI percentage is calculated by analyzing origin marks on text nodes in the document. Text inserted by AI tools is marked at the point of insertion. If an AI-generated passage is modified by more than 20% from its original length, it is reclassified as human-written. Paste events from external sources are tracked separately. The audit trail is frozen at the time of badge generation and cannot be modified.
What is public vs private
Public verification pages show the frozen document snapshot, AI interaction logs used for provenance, paste/session metadata, and computed statistics. Private account data, OAuth credentials, provider secrets, and unpublished drafts are never exposed on the public badge page. Document owners control when a badge is published and can take badges down.
); }
